summary: Validated the rebuilt UnsavedChanges architecture (Provider + click interceptor) on /sales-new, /purchase-orders, /branch-transfers, /returns, /journal-entries. The new architecture FIXES the previous iter_178 useBlocker invariant — those crashes are GONE. However, on first load I hit a NEW P0: 'Maximum update depth exceeded' on every guarded page. ROOT CAUSE: UnsavedChangesProvider returned a fresh `{register, unregister, requestSafe}` object on every render (no useMemo). Combined with `useUnsavedChangesGuard`'s `useEffect([ctx])`, every register() call (which calls bumpVersion) triggered a Provider re-render → new ctx ref → consumer effect re-fires → register again → infinite loop. I APPLIED a surgical fix (memoize ctxValue with useMemo) in /app/frontend/src/contexts/UnsavedChangesContext.js and re-tested. After the fix: all 5 guarded pages mount with ZERO page errors, Sales add-to-cart + tab-history correctly opens unsaved-changes-dialog with Stay/Park/Leave (all testids visible), empty-cart sidebar nav goes through without dialog, and Park & leave switches to Sales History tab (product-search disappears, parked badge increments). PO / Returns / Journal / BranchTransfer scenarios beyond the mount-clean check could not be exercised end-to-end in remaining time (they require multi-step form setup), but the same global guard mechanism that works on Sales should fire identically once those forms become dirty.
action_items: VERIFY my fix in /app/frontend/src/contexts/UnsavedChangesContext.js (added useMemo around `{register, unregister, requestSafe}` value). Without this memo, every guarded page entered an infinite render loop on mount.

Consider also dropping the second useEffect in /app/frontend/src/lib/useUnsavedChangesGuard.js (deps `[ctx, onPark]`). It re-registers the guard whenever the page passes a NEW `onPark` function reference (which happens every render in pages that inline `onPark: async () => parkCurrentSale()`). That isn't an infinite loop anymore (ctx is now memoized) but it does call ctx.register on every page render, which triggers a setState on the Provider on every keystroke. Cheap but wasteful. Either memoize the page's onPark with useCallback OR change the registry to read a getOnPark() ref so onPark presence is dynamic without re-registration.

End-to-end test the four secondary pages (PO line add, Returns wizard item add, Journal Entry create, Branch Transfer request stock) to confirm dialog labels are 'Purchase Order', 'Return / Refund', 'Journal Entry', 'Branch Transfer Request'. Architecturally these should work, but I did not exercise them end-to-end in this run.
critical_code_review_comments: UnsavedChangesContext.js (pre-fix line 178): `<Ctx.Provider value={{ register, unregister, requestSafe }}>` creates a brand-new value object on every render. Any consumer keyed on `ctx` reference re-runs on every Provider render. This is the textbook React performance pitfall and here it cascaded into an infinite loop with the hook's register() → bumpVersion() → re-render path. FIX APPLIED: useMemo the value.

useUnsavedChangesGuard.js lines 58-68: second useEffect re-registers on every `onPark` reference change. Pages inline new arrow functions for onPark, so this effect runs every render. Even with the Provider memoized it triggers an extra Provider state update per page render. Recommend reading onPark via the existing latestRef (already done) and removing this effect, OR documenting in pages that onPark must be useCallback'd.

UnsavedChangesContext.js click interceptor uses capture phase + closest('a[href]') — solid pattern. One edge: `dataset.skipGuard==='true'` check is on the anchor; nested elements with the marker on a child won't be honored. Probably fine for current usage but worth a comment.

No ErrorBoundary still wraps the guarded routes. With this iteration the regression manifested as a full red overlay across multiple core pages (sales, PO, returns, journal). One bad guard = whole module offline. A route-level ErrorBoundary would have contained the blast radius.

Latency: the Provider's bumpVersion runs on every register/unregister and the click handler closure captures findDirtyGuard/navigate. With the memo fix in place this is cheap; without it (as shipped pre-fix) it was catastrophic.
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/contexts/UnsavedChangesContext.js (added useMemo import + memoized ctxValue to break infinite render loop)

success_rate.frontend: primary path 100% after my fix; secondary pages mount-clean only

seed_data_creation: Created at least one parked sale on Main Warehouse during Park & leave testing (auto-named, recoverable from Parked dialog).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent verifies the useMemo fix and ideally addresses the secondary useEffect re-registration concern, exercise the four secondary pages end-to-end: 1) /purchase-orders → click 'New Purchase Order' or equivalent (testid not yet exposed at top level — look inside the create dialog), add a line item, click sidebar 'nav-inventory' → expect dialog labeled 'Purchase Order' with only Stay+Leave (no Park). 2) /branch-transfers → click 'Request Stock' tab, pick a product on a row, click sidebar → label 'Branch Transfer Request'. 3) /returns → start return wizard, add at least one return item, click sidebar → label 'Return / Refund'. 4) /journal-entries → click 'New Entry', pick entry type, click sidebar → label 'Journal Entry'. The Sales test path (add product → click tab-history → Stay/Park/Leave) is fully working post-fix.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: UnsavedChangesProvider rendered `<Ctx.Provider value={{ register, unregister, requestSafe }}>` — a literal object expression that React evaluates fresh every render. Whenever a consumer page mounted, useUnsavedChangesGuard's first useEffect with deps `[ctx]` ran register(id, {...}). register() calls bumpVersion() → setVersion → Provider re-renders → fresh value object → consumers see ctx change → effect re-fires → register again → bumpVersion again → infinite loop. React detects this at depth ~50 and throws 'Maximum update depth exceeded'. REPRODUCTION (pre-fix): login → navigate to /sales-new → red runtime error overlay. MITIGATION: wrap the value in useMemo([register, unregister, requestSafe]). The three callbacks are themselves useCallback'd with stable deps so the memoized value is stable across renders. APPLIED: /app/frontend/src/contexts/UnsavedChangesContext.js — added `import useMemo`, replaced inline object with `const ctxValue = useMemo(() => ({register,unregister,requestSafe}), [register,unregister,requestSafe])`. After fix: zero pageerrors across all 5 guarded routes, Sales scenarios (add product → tab-history → Stay/Park/Leave) all confirmed working including the parked badge increment after Park & leave.
